AUDIT layout consistency in the play flow of an Expo Router app at '/Users/mikhail/Documents/CURSOR CODES/In Progress/backfire'. Scope: app/(app)/play/*.tsx and features/play/** (especially features/play/components/PlayScaffold.tsx and PlayAnswerPanel.tsx), plus app/(app)/game.tsx and app/(app)/game-recap.tsx. Note the game screen forces landscape. For EACH screen record: 1) whether it uses PlayScaffold or hand-rolls layout, 2) safe-area handling and edges, 3) padding/gutter values vs SPACING/LAYOUT tokens in constants/theme.ts, 4) scroll/overflow handling (project rule: flex:1/minHeight:0, ScrollView when content can overflow, density from useWindowDimensions), 5) header/back-button pattern, 6) hardcoded sizes/colors vs tokens, 7) landscape handling differences. Output a compact markdown table per screen plus ranked top inconsistencies with the pattern that should become standard. Read constants/theme.ts and PlayScaffold.tsx first. Do NOT edit anything.

---
**Output:**
Write your findings to exactly this path: /Users/mikhail/Documents/CURSOR CODES/In Progress/backfire/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/43f2c386/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/audit-play-flow.md

text: Creating the output directory and writing the audit document.

content.thinking:
Enough context gathered for the audit. Will grep PlayScaffold usage across play screens and check if game.tsx forces landscape separately from the play flow.

PlayScaffold defaults to full safe area edges and 16-point horizontal padding. play/mode.tsx bypasses this pattern entirely, using a hand-rolled SafeAreaView with GameHeader directly and no ScreenContent wrapper.

play/mode.tsx uses hardcoded tile shadows, font sizes, and content margins instead of theme tokens. It pulls colors from getPlaySurfaceColors() rather than HOME_SOFT_UI. Landscape top margin is handled by isNativeLandscape.

play/quick-length.tsx wraps its layout in SafeAreaView and ScreenContent with full edges and screenGutter padding. It avoids an outer ScrollView and instead uses a flex list with flex:1 option cards.

play/quick-length.tsx hardcodes web margins, gaps, radii, heights, and padding. It uses HOME_SOFT_UI for canvas background and toggles compact mode under 720 pixels.

play/team-setup.tsx uses PlayScaffold with a custom header that omits the hub token chip. It disables body scroll and frame, sets canvas background, and nests an inner ScrollView for classic and rumble layouts. Safe area handling is partial. Landscape uses three-column rows while portrait uses columns. It mixes SPACING tokens with hardcoded radii and modal values.

play/categories.tsx uses PlayScaffold with bodyEdgeToEdge and disabled body scroll and frame. Its custom header is hand-rolled with a back button, counter, title, and random button, omitting the standard PlayStackHeader. A FlatList handles scrolling inside the body. An absolute-positioned floating panel anchors to bottom insets. Landscape triggers a compact header when width exceeds height. Horizontal safe area padding comes from PlayScaffold contentSafeAreaHorizontal.

play/board.tsx also uses PlayScaffold with bodyEdgeToEdge and disabled body frame. It disables contentSafeAreaHorizontal and instead applies manual left and right body padding.

play/board.tsx uses PlayMatchTopBar as its custom header. The grid does not scroll; it fits flexibly with onLayout measurements. Horizontal safe area padding is hand-rolled with Math.max against LAYOUT.screenGutter. Landscape metrics come from getBoardMetrics.

play/question.tsx is fully hand-rolled with no PlayScaffold or SafeAreaView wrapper. It manually manages all four edge insets and uses ScrollView for question and answer phases. PlayMatchTopBar sits alongside custom pill chrome. Scroll areas use flex:1 and minHeight:0. Landscape triggers compact headers based on viewport size.

play/answer.tsx is a thin wrapper. PlayAnswerPanel standalone mode uses PlayScaffold.

text:
Layout audit is written to `.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/43f2c386/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/audit-play-flow.md`.

**Findings in brief:** The play flow uses three layout approaches — `PlayScaffold` (team-setup, categories, board, end, standalone answer), hand-rolled `SafeAreaView` + `GameHeader` (mode, quick-length), and fully hand-rolled match UI (question). Match screens share `PlayMatchTopBar`; setup screens use `PlayStackHeader`/`HubTokenChip`, except team-setup (no token chip) and categories (custom toolbar).

**Top gaps:** (1) question bypasses `PlayScaffold`/`SafeAreaView`, (2) header patterns split three ways, (3) gutters vary (16 vs 20 vs 24 vs ad-hoc), (4) cream soft-UI vs `useTheme()` on end/recap.

**Standard to adopt:** Tier A = default `PlayScaffold` for setup; Tier B = `bodyEdgeToEdge` + `PlayMatchTopBar` + nested scroll only when needed (migrate question here).

```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "Per-screen matrix with file paths, 9 ranked inconsistencies with severity, and recommended Tier A/B standard in audit-play-flow.md"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [],
  "commandsRun": [],
  "validationOutput": [
    "Read-only audit of 16 source files; no code edits"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "question migration to PlayScaffold may regress rumble/hot-seat and sticky dock layouts",
    "Gutter unification may clip web category grid and game header",
    "end/recap cream canvas is a product decision",
    "team-setup logoTitle without token chip may be intentional"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "No code changes; audit markdown artifact only",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "high: app/(app)/play/question.tsx - hand-rolled layout, no PlayScaffold/SafeAreaView",
    "high: play flow - three header patterns (PlayStackHeader, inline GameHeader, PlayMatchTopBar/custom)",
    "high: inconsistent horizontal gutters (LAYOUT.screenGutter 16 vs SPACING.xl 24 vs dynamic %)",
    "medium: team-setup customHeader omits HubTokenChip",
    "medium: end/game-recap use useTheme() not HOME_SOFT_UI canvas",
    "low: game.tsx legacy shell outside play conventions"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "Start with PlayScaffold.tsx then question.tsx for consolidation work. game.tsx and game-recap.tsx are legacy paths using useGameStore / modal pattern."
}
```
